reasoning: Fiserv (FI) has experienced severe negative momentum over the last few months. After reporting slowing growth in its Clover segment in April and May, the stock plunged again on July 23, 2025—its largest single-day decline since 2000—after lowering its full-year organic growth guidance. Following this drop, multiple securities class action lawsuits were filed in late July accusing Fiserv of misleading investors about forced merchant migrations and concealing attrition risks. The deadline for lead plaintiffs is September 22, meaning negative headline risk and regulatory overhang will persist over the near term. This severe fundamental deterioration and legal pressure strongly predict continued short-term downward drift or stagnation below the August 3 price level. For the August 10 resolution, momentum remains highly negative (38%). For September 2, negative pressure from the looming legal deadline keeps the probability depressed (42%). By November 1, after the Q3 earnings report, the stock could potentially see a mean-reverting bounce or new guidance, shifting the probability closer to a coin flip (48%).
